intro: Sketch and Creatie diverge first on pricing model: Sketch uses subscription pricing with no free tier, Creatie uses freemium pricing with a free tier. Everything else flows from that commercial choice. Sketch is built for solo designers and small mac-only teams who prefer native apps over browser tools and is not a fit for distributed teams, windows/linux users, or orgs standardizing on figma. Creatie is built for solo designers and small teams starting fresh projects who want ai-native design workflows and is not a fit for established figma teams with plugins, design systems, and training investments. On the scoring side, Sketch rates 4/5 for developer experience and 5/5 for transparency; Creatie rates 3/5 and 4/5 respectively. The honest trade-off: with Sketch you accept that mac-only limits distributed team adoption, while Creatie comes with the reality that smaller community and plugin ecosystem than figma. Neither side is free. Sketch's headline strength in the data is that still excellent on mac with fast, native performance.
quick_take: Sketch is for solo designers and small mac-only teams who prefer native apps over browser tools; Creatie is for solo designers and small teams starting fresh projects who want ai-native.
verdict_a: Choose Sketch if you are solo designers and small mac-only teams who prefer native apps over browser tools and a subscription pricing model fits how you buy.
verdict_b: Choose Creatie if you are solo designers and small teams starting fresh projects who want ai-native design workflows and a freemium pricing model fits how you buy.

faq.a: Sketch uses a subscription model, while Creatie uses a freemium model; Creatie has a free tier and Sketch does not. We don't have per-seat numbers in this dataset, so 'cheaper' depends on volume. Price both on your actual usage before deciding.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Sketch to Creatie?
faq.a: Sketch has medium lock-in (mac-only, proprietary but export supported); Creatie has medium lock-in (proprietary design tool, exports to figma). Migration effort scales with how much state and automation you've built in Sketch; plan for data export, config rebuild, and a parallel-run period before cutting over.

In our data, Sketch scores 4/5 for developer experience versus 3/5 for Creatie. That said, DX is subjective; the Creatie score may still be acceptable if its feature fit is stronger for your use case.

faq.q: Is Creatie a good alternative to Sketch?
faq.a: Creatie is built for solo designers and small teams starting fresh projects who want ai-native design workflows and explicitly not for established figma teams with plugins, design systems, and training investments, so it's a fit only if your workflow matches its stated audience. If you were using Sketch for solo designers and small mac-only teams who prefer native apps over browser tools, the switch is reasonable; outside that scope, look elsewhere.
